items.id: Amasty Extensions (no specific CVE/APSB for the critical one yet)
items.title: Critical Vulnerability in Amasty Extension leading to Remote Code Execution
items.summary: Sansec reported a massive security release for 25 Amasty extensions, with one critical vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes (CVE-2026-53787) allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, opening the door to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento full-page cache extension, which can lead to remote code execution. CISA added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on June 3, 2026, due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This Adobe Security Bulletin resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.id: PolyShell (no specific CVE/APSB for the initial report)
items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce via REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store using a polyglot technique. Attacks have been observed on a high percentage of stores, and while Adobe released a fix in Magento 2.4.9, older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization Bypass in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This vulnerability allows a low-privileged attacker to bypass security measures and gain limited unauthorized view access of data in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. It is classified as an Incorrect Authorization issue (CWE-863).

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update addressing multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. These vulnerabilities, including incorrect authorization (CVE-2026-21284), improper access control (CVE-2026-21285), stored XSS (CVE-2026-21310), and path traversal (CVE-2026-21293), could lead to full system takeover, data leaks, admin hijacking, and file exposure.
